name: Calcimycin
scope_note: An ionophorous, polyether antibiotic from Streptomyces chartreusensis. It binds and transports CALCIUM and other divalent cations across membranes and uncouples oxidative phosphorylation while inhibiting ATPase of rat liver mitochondria. The substance is used mostly as a biochemical tool to study the role of divalent cations in various biological systems.

name: Temefos
scope_note: An organothiophosphate insecticide.
annotation: for use to kill or control insects, use no qualifiers on the insecticide or the insect; appropriate qualifiers may be used when other aspects of the insecticide are discussed such as the effect on a physiologic process or behavioral aspect of the insect; for poisoning, coordinate with ORGANOPHOSPHATE POISONING

name: Abattoirs
scope_note: Places where animals are slaughtered and dressed for market.

name: Abbreviations as Topic
scope_note: Works about shortened forms of written words or phrases used for brevity.
annotation: includes acronyms; do not confuse with Publication Type ABBREVIATIONS

name: Abdomen
scope_note: That portion of the body that lies between the THORAX and the PELVIS.
annotation: general or unspecified; prefer specifics; ABDOMINAL MUSCLES and specifics are available; abrupt disease requiring emergency surgery equals ABDOMEN, ACUTE

name: Abdomen, Acute
scope_note: A clinical syndrome with acute abdominal pain that is severe, localized, and rapid in onset. Acute abdomen may be caused by a variety of disorders, injuries, or diseases.

name: Abdominal Injuries
scope_note: General or unspecified injuries involving organs in the abdominal cavity.
annotation: general term for inj of organs in the abdom cavity; prefer specific organs /inj

name: Abdominal Neoplasms
scope_note: New abnormal growth of tissue in the ABDOMEN.
annotation: general term for neoplasms of organs in the abdominal cavity; prefer specific organ/neoplasm terms; coordinate IM with histological type of neoplasm if given (IM)

name: Abdominal Muscles
scope_note: Muscles forming the ABDOMINAL WALL including RECTUS ABDOMINIS; ABDOMINAL OBLIQUE MUSCLES, transversus abdominis, pyramidalis muscles and quadratus abdominis.
annotation: /growth: coordinate with MUSCLE DEVELOPMENT; /embryol: coordinate with MUSCLE DEVELOPMENT if pertinent

name: Abducens Nerve
scope_note: The 6th cranial nerve which originates in the ABDUCENS NUCLEUS of the PONS and sends motor fibers to the lateral rectus muscles of the EYE. Damage to the nerve or its nucleus disrupts horizontal eye movement control.
annotation: neoplasms = ABDUCENS NERVE DISEASES (IM) + CRANIAL NERVE NEOPLASMS (IM) + histological type of neoplasm (IM)

name: Abelson murine leukemia virus
scope_note: A replication-defective strain of Murine leukemia virus (LEUKEMIA VIRUS, MURINE) capable of transforming lymphoid cells and producing a rapidly progressing lymphoid leukemia after superinfection with FRIEND MURINE LEUKEMIA VIRUS; MOLONEY MURINE LEUKEMIA VIRUS; or RAUSCHER VIRUS.
annotation: infection: coordinate with RETROVIRIDAE INFECTIONS + TUMOR VIRUS INFECTIONS + LEUKEMIA, EXPERIMENTAL

name: Abetalipoproteinemia
entry_terms: Microsomal Triglyceride Transfer Protein Deficiency Disease
scope_note: An autosomal recessive disorder of lipid metabolism. It is caused by mutation of the microsomal triglyceride transfer protein that catalyzes the transport of lipids (TRIGLYCERIDES; CHOLESTEROL ESTERS; PHOSPHOLIPIDS) and is required in the secretion of BETA-LIPOPROTEINS (low density lipoproteins or LDL). Features include defective intestinal lipid absorption, very low serum cholesterol level, and near absent LDL.

name: Congenital Abnormalities
scope_note: Malformations of organs or body parts during development in utero.
annotation: general only; prefer specifics or /abnorm with specific anatomical term or specific animal

name: Abnormalities, Drug-Induced
scope_note: Congenital abnormalities caused by medicinal substances or drugs of abuse given to or taken by the mother, or to which she is inadvertently exposed during the manufacture of such substances. The concept excludes abnormalities resulting from exposure to non-medicinal chemicals in the environment.
annotation: IM; do not use /chem ind /compl /congen & do not coord with INFANT, NEWBORN, DISEASES; coord IM with specific chem /adv eff-pois-tox (IM) + specific organ /abnorm or specific abnorm /chem ind (IM); Manual 23.20.3

name: Abnormalities, Multiple
scope_note: Congenital abnormalities that affect more than one organ or body structure.
annotation: coordinate with specific abnormalities

name: Abnormalities, Radiation-Induced
scope_note: Congenital changes in the morphology of organs produced by exposure to ionizing or non-ionizing radiation.
annotation: coordinate with specific abnormality

name: ABO Blood-Group System
scope_note: The major human blood type system which depends on the presence or absence of two antigens A and B. Type O occurs when neither A nor B is present and AB when both are present. A and B are genetic factors that determine the presence of enzymes for the synthesis of certain glycoproteins mainly in the red cell membrane.
annotation: IM; coord with disease /blood (IM), not /immunol nor /genet; TN 230

name: Abomasum
scope_note: The fourth stomach of ruminating animals. It is also called the true stomach. It is an elongated pear-shaped sac lying on the floor of the abdomen, on the right-hand side, and roughly between the seventh and twelfth ribs. It leads to the beginning of the small intestine. (From Black's Veterinary Dictionary, 17th ed)
annotation: a ruminant's 4th stomach; dis: coord IM with STOMACH DISEASES /vet (IM); neopl: coord IM with STOMACH NEOPLASMS /vet (IM) + histol type /vet (IM); ulcer: coord IM with STOMACH ULCER /vet (IM)

name: Abortifacient Agents
scope_note: Chemical substances that interrupt pregnancy after implantation.
annotation: use no qualifiers for abortifacients when used to interrupt pregnancy; note entry term ABORTIFACIENT EFFECT: use only for the abortifacient effect of a chemical substance

name: Abortifacient Agents, Nonsteroidal
scope_note: Non-steroidal chemical compounds with abortifacient activity.
annotation: do not use /ther use for use of the abortifacients as abortifacients

name: Abortifacient Agents, Steroidal
scope_note: Steroidal compounds with abortifacient activity.
annotation: do not use /ther use for use of the abortifacients as abortifacients

name: Abortion, Spontaneous
scope_note: Expulsion of the product of FERTILIZATION before completing the term of GESTATION and without deliberate interference.
annotation: /chem ind permitted but do not confuse with ABORTION, INDUCED; check the tags HUMANS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion Applicants
scope_note: Individuals requesting induced abortions.
annotation: don't forget also check tags HUMANS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Illegal
scope_note: Illegal termination of pregnancy.
annotation: don't forget also check tags HUMANS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY; for abortifacients in crim abort see note on ABORTION, INDUCED

name: Abortion, Eugenic
scope_note: Abortion performed because of possible fetal defects.
annotation: add HUMANS or ANIMALS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Habitual
scope_note: Three or more consecutive spontaneous abortions.

name: Abortion, Incomplete
scope_note: Premature loss of PREGNANCY in which not all the products of CONCEPTION have been expelled.
annotation: restrict to spontaneous, not induced abortions; check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Induced
scope_note: Intentional removal of a fetus from the uterus by any of a number of techniques. (POPLINE, 1978)
annotation: for use of an abortifacient, coordinate with abortifacient agent with no qualifiers, not /adv eff unless text specifically discusses adverse effects; check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY
history_note: 71; was see under ABORTION, CRIMINAL & ABORTION, THERAPEUTIC 1963-70, was see under ABORTION, LEGAL 1964-70; EMBRYOTOMY was heading 1963-85

name: Abortion, Legal
scope_note: Termination of pregnancy under conditions allowed under local laws. (POPLINE Thesaurus, 1991)
annotation: add HUMANS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Missed
scope_note: The retention in the UTERUS of a dead FETUS two months or more after its DEATH.
annotation: check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Septic
scope_note: Any type of abortion, induced or spontaneous, that is associated with infection of the UTERUS and its appendages. It is characterized by FEVER, uterine tenderness, and foul discharge.
annotation: /vet permitted only if discussed as "septic" abortion: do not use automatically for animal abortion in brucellosis, vibriosis, trichomoniasis, etc; check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Therapeutic
scope_note: Abortion induced to save the life or health of a pregnant woman. (From Dorland, 28th ed)
annotation: check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Threatened
scope_note: UTERINE BLEEDING from a GESTATION of less than 20 weeks without any CERVICAL DILATATION. It is characterized by vaginal bleeding, lower back discomfort, or midline pelvic cramping and a risk factor for MISCARRIAGE.
annotation: check the tags FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abortion, Veterinary
scope_note: Premature expulsion of the FETUS in animals.
annotation: coordinate IM with specific type of abortion /vet (IM); check the tags ANIMALS & FEMALE & PREGNANCY

name: Abreaction
scope_note: A process in psychotherapy in which the patient is desensitized to emotionally painful, often forgotten (repressed) memories by recalling and reacting to them in the safety of the treatment setting.

name: Abrin
scope_note: A toxic lectin from the seeds of jequirity, Abrus precatorius L. Very active poison. Five different proteins have so far been isolated: Abrus agglutinin, the component responsible for: hemagglutinating activity, & abrins a-d, the toxic principals each consisting of two peptide chains are held together by disulfide bonds.

name: Abruptio Placentae
scope_note: Premature separation of the normally implanted PLACENTA from the UTERUS. Signs of varying degree of severity include UTERINE BLEEDING, uterine MUSCLE HYPERTONIA, and FETAL DISTRESS or FETAL DEATH.

name: Abscess
scope_note: Accumulation of purulent material in tissues, organs, or circumscribed spaces, usually associated with signs of infection.
annotation: coordinate with site (organ/disease term)  + precoordinated organism/infection term; if unspecified bacterial abscess do not coordinate with BACTERIAL INFECTIONS (since most abscesses are bacterial) but coordinate with specific bacterial infection term if given (as ABSCESS + STAPHYLOCOCCAL INFECTIONS)

name: Peritonsillar Abscess
scope_note: An accumulation of purulent material in the area between the PALATINE TONSIL and its capsule.
annotation: coordinate IM with infection term (IM) but not BACTERIAL INFECTIONS (since most abscesses are bacterial)

name: Abscisic Acid
scope_note: Abscission-accelerating plant growth substance isolated from young cotton fruit, leaves of sycamore, birch, and other plants, and from potatoes, lemons, avocados, and other fruits.

name: Absenteeism
scope_note: Chronic absence from work or other duty.
history_note: was in Cat F & J 1967-82, was in Cat F & I 1963-66

name: Absorption
scope_note: The physical or physiological processes by which substances, tissue, cells, etc. take up or take in other substances or energy.
annotation: beginning in 2015: used for searching; INDEXER: Do not use; CATALOGER: Do not use

name: Abstracting and Indexing
scope_note: Works about activities performed to identify concepts and aspects of published information and research reports.
annotation: medical abstracting & indexing: do not coordinate with MEDICINE; do not confuse with Publication Types ABSTRACTS or MEETING ABSTRACT
history_note: 2008(1963); for INDEXES as topic use REFERENCE BOOKS 1996-1998

name: Dental Abutments
scope_note: Natural teeth or teeth roots used as anchorage for a fixed or removable denture or other prosthesis (such as an implant) serving the same purpose.

name: Acacia
scope_note: A plant genus of the family FABACEAE. The gums and tanning agents obtained from Acacia are called GUM ARABIC. The common name catechu is more often used for Areca catechu (ARECA).
annotation: acacia pharmaceut agents are probably GUM ARABIC
history_note: 91(86)63; was see under TREES 1986-90; was heading 1963-85

name: Academic Medical Centers
scope_note: Medical complexes consisting of medical school, hospitals, clinics, libraries, administrative facilities, etc.
annotation: specify geog

name: Academies and Institutes
scope_note: Organizations representing specialized fields which are accepted as authoritative; may be non-governmental, university or an independent research organization, e.g., National Academy of Sciences, Brookings Institution, etc.
annotation: not for educ acad & institutes ( = SCHOOLS or its indentions); Acad of Med: do not index also under MEDICINE; NY Acad of Med: when geog coord needed use NEW YORK CITY; DF: ACADEMIES

name: Acanthamoeba
scope_note: A genus of free-living soil amoebae that produces no flagellate stage. Its organisms are pathogens for several infections in humans and have been found in the eye, bone, brain, and respiratory tract.
annotation: infection: coordinate with AMEBIASIS; for encephalitis caused by this organism, coordinate also with CENTRAL NERVOUS SYSTEM PROTOZOAL INFECTIONS + INFECTIOUS ENCEPHALITIS; coordinate with specific protozoan terms (ANTIGENS, PROTOZOAN, etc.) if pertinent

name: Acanthocephala
scope_note: A phylum of parasitic worms, closely related to tapeworms and containing two genera: Moniliformis, which sometimes infects man, and Macracanthorhynchus, which infects swine.
annotation: infection: coordinate with HELMINTHIASIS or HELMINTHIASIS, ANIMAL; coordinate with specific helminth terms (ANTIGENS, HELMINTH, etc.) if pertinent

name: Acanthocytes
scope_note: Erythrocytes with protoplasmic projections giving the cell a thorny appearance.

name: Acantholysis
scope_note: Separation of the prickle cells of the stratum spinosum of the epidermis, resulting in atrophy of the prickle cell layer. It is seen in diseases such as pemphigus vulgaris (see PEMPHIGUS) and DARIER DISEASE.
annotation: ACANTHOLYSIS BULLOSA see EPIDERMOLYSIS BULLOSA is also available

name: Acanthosis Nigricans
scope_note: A circumscribed melanosis consisting of a brown-pigmented, velvety verrucosity or fine papillomatosis appearing in the axillae and other body folds. It occurs in association with endocrine disorders, underlying malignancy, administration of certain drugs, or as in inherited disorder.
annotation: a type of melanosis

name: Acari
scope_note: A large, subclass of arachnids comprising the MITES and TICKS, including parasites of plants, animals, and humans, as well as several important disease vectors.

name: Acceleration
scope_note: An increase in the rate of speed.
annotation: IM

name: Accessory Nerve
scope_note: The 11th cranial nerve which originates from NEURONS in the MEDULLA and in the CERVICAL SPINAL CORD. It has a cranial root, which joins the VAGUS NERVE (10th cranial) and sends motor fibers to the muscles of the LARYNX, and a spinal root, which sends motor fibers to the TRAPEZIUS and the sternocleidomastoid muscles.
annotation: neoplasm = ACCESSORY NERVE DISEASES (IM) + CRANIAL NERVE NEOPLASMS (IM) + histological type of neoplasm (IM)

name: Accident Prevention
scope_note: Efforts and designs to reduce the incidence of unexpected undesirable events in various environments and situations.

name: Accident Proneness
scope_note: Tendency toward involvement in accidents. Implies certain personality characteristics which predispose to accidents.

name: Accidental Falls
scope_note: Falls due to slipping or tripping which may result in injury.

name: Accidents
scope_note: Unexpected and unintentional events, typically resulting in ACCIDENTAL INJURIES.
annotation: general or unspecified; prefer specifics

name: Accidents, Aviation
scope_note: Unanticipated events associated with AIRCRAFT.

name: Accidents, Home
scope_note: ACCIDENTS which occur within the home.

name: Accidents, Occupational
scope_note: Unforeseen occurrences, especially injuries in the course of work-related activities.
annotation: coordinate IM with specific occupation (IM)
history_note: 79; was in Cat G & J 1979-82, was ACCIDENTS, INDUSTRIAL in Cat G & J 1967-78 & Cat G & I 1963-66

name: Accidents, Traffic
scope_note: Accidents on streets, roads, and highways involving drivers, passengers, pedestrians, or vehicles. Traffic accidents refer to AUTOMOBILES (passenger cars, buses, and trucks), BICYCLING, and MOTORCYCLES but not OFF-ROAD MOTOR VEHICLES; RAILROADS nor snowmobiles.
annotation: implies automobile accidents so do not coordinate with AUTOMOBILES unless the auto is especially discussed; coordinate with BICYCLING or MOTORCYCLES if pertinent; not for railroad accidents ( = RAILROADS + ACCIDENTS) or all-terrain vehicles, motorbikes or snowmobiles ( = OFF-ROAD MOTOR VEHICLES + ACCIDENTS)

name: Acclimatization
scope_note: Adaptation to a new environment or to a change in the old.
annotation: includes to climate, altitude, weather but not to gravity ( = ADAPTATION, PHYSIOLOGICAL)

name: Accommodation, Ocular
scope_note: The dioptric adjustment of the EYE (to attain maximal sharpness of retinal imagery for an object of regard) referring to the ability, to the mechanism, or to the process. Ocular accommodation is the effecting of refractive changes by changes in the shape of the CRYSTALLINE LENS. Loosely, it refers to ocular adjustments for VISION, OCULAR at various distances. (Cline et al., Dictionary of Visual Science, 4th ed)
annotation: do not confuse with ADAPTATION, OCULAR, adjustment of eye to light

name: Accounting
scope_note: System of recording financial transactions.
annotation: coordinate with specialty or specific financial endeavor

name: Accounts Payable and Receivable
scope_note: Short-term debt obligations and assets occurring in the regular course of operational transactions.

name: Accreditation
scope_note: Certification as complying with a standard set by non-governmental organizations, applied for by institutions, programs, and facilities on a voluntary basis.
annotation: by the profession; do not confuse with LICENSURE (by the state)

name: Acculturation
scope_note: Process of cultural change in which one group or members of a group assimilate various cultural patterns from another.

name: Acebutolol
scope_note: A cardioselective beta-1 adrenergic antagonist with little effect on the bronchial receptors. The drug has stabilizing and quinidine-like effects on cardiac rhythm, as well as weak inherent sympathomimetic action.

name: Acedapsone
scope_note: Acetylated sulfone that is slowly metabolized to give long-term, low blood levels of DAPSONE. It has antimicrobial and antimalarial action, but is mainly used as a depot leprostatic agent.

name: Acenaphthenes
scope_note: Tricyclic ethylene-bridged naphthalene derivatives. They are found in petroleum residues and coal tar and used as dye intermediates, in the manufacture of plastics, and in insecticides and fungicides.

name: Acenocoumarol
scope_note: A coumarin that is used as an anticoagulant. Its actions and uses are similar to those of WARFARIN. (From Martindale, The Extra Pharmacopoeia, 30th ed, p233)

name: Acepromazine
scope_note: A phenothiazine that is used in the treatment of PSYCHOSES.

name: Acetabularia
scope_note: A genus of green algae found in the Mediterranean and other warm seas.

name: Acetabulum
scope_note: The part of the pelvis that comprises the pelvic socket where the head of FEMUR joins to form HIP JOINT (acetabulofemoral joint).
annotation: point of union of the 3 pelvic bones; do not confuse with HIP or HIP JOINT; fractures = ACETABULUM /inj (IM) + FRACTURES, BONE (IM);  /surg: consider also ACETABULOPLASTY

name: Acetaldehyde
scope_note: A colorless, flammable liquid used in the manufacture of acetic acid, perfumes, and flavors. It is also an intermediate in the metabolism of alcohol. It has a general narcotic action and also causes irritation of mucous membranes. Large doses may cause death from respiratory paralysis.

name: Acetals
scope_note: Diethers with the structure -C(OR'')(OR'''), where R'' and  R''' are not hydrogen. Mixed acetals have R'' and R''' groups which differ.

name: Acetamides
scope_note: Derivatives of acetamide that are used as solvents, as mild irritants, and in organic synthesis.

name: Acetaminophen
scope_note: Analgesic antipyretic derivative of acetanilide. It has weak anti-inflammatory properties and is used as a common analgesic, but may cause liver, blood cell, and kidney damage.

name: Acetanilides
scope_note: Compounds based on N-phenylacetamide, that are similar in structure to 2-PHENYLACETAMIDES. They are precursors of many other compounds. They were formerly used as ANALGESICS and ANTIPYRETICS, but often caused lethal METHEMOGLOBINEMIA.

name: Acetate Kinase
scope_note: An enzyme that catalyzes reversibly the phosphorylation of acetate in the presence of a divalent cation and ATP with the formation of acetylphosphate and ADP. It is important in the glycolysis process. EC 2.7.2.1.

name: Acetates
scope_note: Derivatives of ACETIC ACID. Included under this heading are a broad variety of acid forms, salts, esters, and amides that contain the carboxymethane structure.
annotation: general or unspecified; prefer specifics

name: Acetazolamide
scope_note: One of the CARBONIC ANHYDRASE INHIBITORS that is sometimes effective against absence seizures. It is sometimes useful also as an adjunct in the treatment of tonic-clonic, myoclonic, and atonic seizures, particularly in women whose seizures occur or are exacerbated at specific times in the menstrual cycle. However, its usefulness is transient often because of rapid development of tolerance. Its antiepileptic effect may be due to its inhibitory effect on brain carbonic anhydrase, which leads to an increased transneuronal chloride gradient, increased chloride current, and increased inhibition. (From Smith and Reynard, Textbook of Pharmacology, 1991, p337)

name: Acetic Anhydrides
scope_note: Compounds used extensively as acetylation, oxidation and dehydrating agents and in the modification of proteins and enzymes.

name: Acetoacetates
scope_note: Salts and derivatives of acetoacetic acid.

name: Acetobacter
scope_note: A species of gram-negative bacteria of the family ACETOBACTERACEAE found in FLOWERS and FRUIT. Cells are ellipsoidal to rod-shaped and straight or slightly curved.
annotation: do not confuse with ACETOBACTERIUM

name: Acetohexamide
scope_note: A sulfonylurea hypoglycemic agent that is metabolized in the liver to 1-hydrohexamide.

name: Acetoin
scope_note: A product of fermentation. It is a component of the butanediol cycle in microorganisms. In mammals it is oxidized to carbon dioxide.

name: Acetoin Dehydrogenase
scope_note: An enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of acetoin to diacetyl in the presence of NAD.

name: Acetolactate Synthase
scope_note: A flavoprotein enzyme that catalyzes the formation of acetolactate from 2 moles of PYRUVATE in the biosynthesis of VALINE and the formation of acetohydroxybutyrate from pyruvate and alpha-ketobutyrate in the biosynthesis of ISOLEUCINE. This enzyme was formerly listed as EC 4.1.3.18.

name: Acetone
scope_note: A colorless liquid used as a solvent and an antiseptic. It is one of the ketone bodies produced during ketoacidosis.
annotation: ACETONEMIA and ACETONURIA see KETOSIS are also available

name: Acetonitriles
scope_note: Compounds in which a methyl group is attached to the cyano moiety.

name: Acetophenones
scope_note: Derivatives of the simplest aromatic ketone acetophenone (of general formula C6H5C(O)CH3).

name: Acetoxyacetylaminofluorene
scope_note: An alkylating agent that forms DNA ADDUCTS at the C-8 position in GUANINE, resulting in single strand breaks. It has demonstrated carcinogenic action.
history_note: 91(75); was see under 2-ACETYLAMINOFLUORENE/analogs & derivatives 1980-90; was see under FLUORENES 1975-79

name: Acetrizoic Acid
scope_note: An iodinated radiographic contrast medium used as acetrizoate sodium in HYSTEROSALPINGOGRAPHY.

name: Acetyl-CoA C-Acetyltransferase
scope_note: An enzyme that catalyzes the formation of acetoacetyl-CoA from two molecules of ACETYL COA. Some enzymes called thiolase or thiolase-I have referred to this activity or to the activity of ACETYL-COA C-ACYLTRANSFERASE.
annotation: do not confuse with ACETYL-COA C-ACYLTRANSFERASE

name: Acetyl-CoA C-Acyltransferase
scope_note: Enzyme that catalyzes the final step of fatty acid oxidation in which ACETYL COA is released and the CoA ester of a fatty acid two carbons shorter is formed.
annotation: do not confuse with ACETYL-COA C-ACETYLTRANSFERASE

name: Acetyl-CoA Carboxylase
scope_note: A carboxylating enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of ATP, acetyl-CoA, and HCO3- to ADP, orthophosphate, and malonyl-CoA. It is a biotinyl-protein that also catalyzes transcarboxylation. The plant enzyme also carboxylates propanoyl-CoA and butanoyl-CoA (From Enzyme Nomenclature, 1992) EC 6.4.1.2.

name: Acetyl-CoA Hydrolase
scope_note: An enzyme that catalyzes reversibly the hydrolysis of acetyl-CoA to yield CoA and acetate. The enzyme is involved in the oxidation of fatty acids. EC 3.1.2.1.
